comments.text:
BEWARE of NIVA BUPA.....They are the most FRAUDESTER company in Medicla insurance. They will never pay you if your claim amount is high. Recently my mother (68 years) got heart attack and treatment was taken in Fortis Hospital but they rejected my claim asking that once she had Arthritis 22 years ago and they need that Prescription of Doctor as hard copy. I explained that at time she had taken medication only for a month, she got relief and never has taken medication since then, also how can any one keep a Doctors prescription for 22 years, but they were adament on asking the 22 years ago prescription and they rejected my claim of 4.5 lacs on that ground. Infact with each day and my follow up they began to raise new request asking for Diabetes First prescription given by Dr. in 2015 (however diabetes was declared in policy), asking for Dengue prescription once she had in 2009.
I visited their office and found people that they settle big claims 1/100 otherwise they raise query in 99% cases of big amount. If you claim amount is equal or twice the premium amount you are paying then it is okay for them to settle the claim, but if your claim amount is higher or equal to policy limit then even GOD will not save you from NIVA BUPA.
They know that my mother is in elderly age and the claim of 4,50 lacs will be paid in 8 premium amount and that too if every thing goes okay with my mother in future, as such playing on a safer side NIVA BUPA choose the way to escape by raising unrealistic query to my claim.
After a long harrassment by them I asked them to cancel my policy and give my premium balance left in a year ( I paid the premium thrice before for same policy of RS. 65000 every year), but they denied this refund also to me. If any one had any doubt I can present the proof to them. 
So, I decided to expose them on every social platform so that no other person shall get befooled by them.
I will recommend people that if your claim is twice the limit of your premium then it is okay for the company to pay you but if you think that your policy is of 15 lacs and you go to the Hospital thinking you have nothing to pay as your Insurance company will take care of you, then you are COMPLETELY WRONG. You have to pay from your pocket and you will be Debt. So instead of paying these company I wil recommend to save the premium amount to your bankers and get them Fixed deposit and use it when required. In either case you have to suffer then why to suffer because of some third person as you will feel more robbed as you have paid twice once you had paid to insurance company and secondly you have paid to Hospital.
Please BEWARE of NIVA BUPA

comments.text:
I believe that your interpretation of the HDFC's restore benefit was not correct. The restoration will be done only once, and the resultant "base sum insured" will be available for future claims. So, if the first claim was of Rs2, then only Rs2 will be restored, and that's it, no more restoration. This makes ICICI's restoration better.

Similarly, the interpretation of ICICI's Infinite Care's point iv was not correct IMO. What that means is, once you have claimed Infinite Care, then no future claims (in that year) are possible because sum insured has become zero.

comments.text: Personally, even if Public Insurance Companies has ICR above 100%, it is still safe as it will be funded by govt. I have 20 lakh corporate insurance at New India Insurance, and me as well as my other colleagues haven't faced that much issue. We have room rent limit of 10,000 per day and co-pay of 85% in our corporate insurance, but its mainly non-issue for most, we are careful in selecting which room and all. Even if higher rent room is selected, the claims still get passed based on room rent limit calculation,and  only the extra amount goes out of our pocket. This is mentioned in our policy document as well. Issues like slow claim processing and co-payment amount can be issue for some, but at the end of the most claims get passed without any stupid rejection reason. One of my colleague who had Orient Insurance even forgot to intimate the TPA and  sent the notice two days late, he was given the flexibility and his claim was still passed. Private Company would have rejected then and there, though this is a valid reason for rejection.

comments.text:
very nice informative video as usual by @LabourLawAdvisor team. I purchased the health insurance right after watching this video.
However, I checked with policy bazaar agent for "infinite care" rider in ICICI Elevate plan,  the term " After utilization of all the above mentioned Sum Insured, the Total Sum Insured shall be reduced to zero for that Policy Year following the payment of claim under Infinite Care.". This does NOT mean that we need to first exhaust all the available sum insured to Zero in earlier claims before the claim under infinite care. This term simply means that if you claim under infinite claim then all the available sum insured will become zero for that year. This is obvious as the infinite claim amount is very large so it will be paid step by step from available all sum insured and then additional it will be paid from infinite care. So, this rider seems to be useful. May be you can reverify and confirm. Thanks.

comments.text:
There's some things he missed about aggregate deductible. 

An aggregate deductible is the amount a policyholder must pay before insurance covers claims. For example, with a policy having a 1 lakh aggregate deductible and a 10 lakh cover, the insurance pays only after you cover the first 1 lakh out of pocket (or from another plan like corporate or group).

This option reduces premiums but means you'll pay a significant amount yourself in case of claims. Proof (bills) needs to be submitted to the insurer that you paid ₹50000 out of your own pocket and you did not use your company's policy to cover that amount. The additional documentation and approval times vary depending on the insurer.

Additionally, the deductible can only be reduced or waived once, subject to the following conditions:
The oldest insured must be under 50 when buying the policy, it must be held continuously for at least 5 years, and the oldest insured must be under 61 when reducing the deductible (for hdfc ergo).

Dear Rishabh and Labour Law Advisor Team,

Greetings! I’m a big fan of your channel and the way you break down complex labour law topics for us. I’m reaching out with a request for a video addressing a growing issue in the corporate world that’s affecting many employees, including myself.

There’s a scam-like trend where companies hire people with just verbal confirmation—no offer letter—leaving us vulnerable. Once inside, we face delayed salaries (sometimes paid between the 6th and 25th, or not in full), no weekly offs, mandatory unpaid overtime, and threats like “resign if you don’t like it” when we object. These firms hold our salaries as leverage, knowing we can’t fight back without risking financial instability. It’s mental torture, stripping away our freedom to work or manage finances fairly.

Could you please make a detailed video criticizing these exploitative companies, explaining the labour laws they’re violating (like offer letter mandates, timely payment rules, and overtime pay rights), and sharing practical steps employees can take to avoid or escape these traps? Your insights could empower so many stuck in these situations.

Thank you for considering this—it’d mean a lot to your viewers facing this daily grind. Looking forward to your thoughts!

Kind regards,

It's not about watching this video and rushing to buy an HDFC Ergo health insurance policy just because the LLA team spoke positively about it. Consumers today are much more informed and capable of making their own decisions based on the core knowledge they gather from various sources, including such videos.

What I appreciated about this video is how it breaks down the complexities of health insurance policies and highlights the critical aspects consumers should be aware of before making a purchase. Understanding policy wordings, exclusions, and hidden clauses is crucial, and this video does a great job of educating viewers about these nuances.

If someone blindly trusts any YouTube content creator without doing their own research, that is their mistake. As a consumer, my objective is to gain knowledge and use it wisely to make an informed decision when selecting the right health insurance plan

comments.text: Insurance Industry especially in India has a long way to mature. Lack of regulation & too much leeway is awarded to the Insurance Companies for Rejecting claims even when they are challenged in Courts. There is no Set Standard policy on treating illnesses / approving claims - it's always left to the discretion of the Companies depending on the value of the claim. Overall, Insurance Companies yet to garner enough TRUST from Consumers when all they do is pay up lower amount claims & reject high value claims for stupid reasons. Hiring Private Investigators to look for reason to Reject Claims of Individuals is a step too far but is openly happening in almost all of the Headline companies. Insurance Premiums have to mature / come down in line with International Standards Insured Amount to Premium Ratio is one of the Highest in the World. Everyone knows all of these deceitful tactics and Average Public is not a fool to keep paying sky high premiums only to get their claim rejected when needed = ultimately making the Insurance companies RICH. That's the reason why Insurance as an Industry isn't picking up in INDIA even after so much peddling from YouTube so called influencers.

This video offers an ultimate guide on how to find the best health insurance in India for 2025. It emphasizes the importance of choosing the right health insurance company.
Here's a breakdown:

 * Choosing the Right Plan: The video discusses whether to opt for an individual plan or a family floater, advising against combining parents in the same plan due to premium costs [48:50].

comments.text: The basis of that you choose for choosing best insurer using icr I don’t think is apt because if icr is 100% or more that means that the co. Is paying more than its earned premium that doesn’t imply that they will reject claims or is not a good insurance co. For choosing an insurer one should look for which company has good policy with good features and choose on the basis of it purely. As you know that many govt companies have high icr it’s because most of the claims are passed and paid that’s why they might be a good co. Who looks after social welfare rather than profitability compared to a private player. So choosing a best policy with good features and companies which have a good reputation in the market should be choosen be it govt or private.

I've a story with Star Health, and after that I'm thinking to go with HDFC ERGO, but after this what should I choose??? How can I rely in a Health Insurance. 
I really don't like this Star Health Insurance, here is a real life incident with me:
On 2nd August evening I admitted my father to Narayana Hospital he was having blood vomiting. I don't know does Narayana took their patient i.e. my father seriously my father is just in Emergency Care for atleast 3 to 4 hours. l've a Insurance policy with Star and with Narayana there is a cashless claim available. After getting out from the Emergency Care my father is admitted in lCU but I don't know how they care my father's health. Might this is a cashless claim
(treatment) so they don't give much importance. Though everything is okay but on 3rd August my father left me, he died. Ijust don't know who have the fault, previous night I done conversation with my father and in just next day morning he is in a serious condition, I have doubt in Narayana as well as Star Health (due to there low and late claim settlement). I have nothing to do further and in a age of 17 I lost my father.
My father died at 11.30 am and I got him back at 5 pm just because Star Health didn't done the settlement. Besides of that the hospitality
bill was around 1 Lakhs and Star just settled 88K something, and we have to pay the amount in Cash and then I got my father.
After that incident I just lost every belive in Star as well as Narayana Health. I am going to switch in in HDFC ERGO Optima Secure next year as in Star early premium is paid already. With higher premium if get some better benifit migth that is the best for me. Please help me to choose the right one.
